sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Celta Vigo, currently sitting in 8th place with 46 points, host Sevilla, who are struggling in 15th place with 38 points, at the Balaídos Stadium in Vigo. This La Liga clash promises to be intriguing given the disparity in league positions. The referee, Pablo Gonzalez Fuertes, is known for a high average of 5.5 yellow cards per game, which could influence the physicality of this encounter. Both teams will be eager to secure vital points as the season progresses.

sections.subtitle: Recent Form and Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two sides, the results are evenly balanced with 1 win each for Celta Vigo and Sevilla, and 3 draws. Their most recent encounter on December 14, 2024, saw Sevilla edge out a 1-0 victory at home, with Saúl Ñíguez earning the Player of the Match title with a 7.9 Sofascore rating. Celta Vigo have been involved in high-scoring games recently, with 4 of their last 5 home matches featuring over 2.5 goals. Sevilla, however, are on a poor run with no wins in their last 7 away games and no clean sheets in the same span.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Celta Vigo
sections.text: Celta Vigo will rely heavily on their standout forward Iago Aspas, who boasts a 7.23 average Sofascore rating and has contributed 9 goals and 4 assists this season. Borja Iglesias, in fine form over the last 5 matches with a 7.55 average rating and 4 goals, will be another crucial asset up front. Óscar Mingueza, with an impressive 7.06 rating and 6 assists, provides creativity from midfield. Young talent Hugo Álvarez, with 4 goals and 3 assists, could also make an impact in this game.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Sevilla
sections.text: Sevilla’s attack will hinge on Dodi Lukebakio, who ranks 3rd in the league for successful dribbles per game at 2.4, and has notched 11 goals this season with a 7.08 rating. Saúl Ñíguez, a consistent performer with a 7.0 rating and 6 assists, will be vital in midfield. Juanlu Sánchez, with 4 goals and 4 assists, offers a dual threat from midfield. Defender Kike Salas, with a steady 6.97 rating, will aim to anchor a defense that has struggled for clean sheets.

sections.subtitle: Team Statistics and Tactical Insights
sections.text: Celta Vigo rank 5th in the league for total goals scored with 52, and also 5th for big chances created with 82, showcasing their attacking prowess. Their possession game is strong, ranking 5th with an average of 53.74%, and they excel in accurate passes with an 85.34% rate, ranking 4th. However, defensively, they struggle, ranking 17th for goals conceded with 52. Sevilla tend to score late, with 21.74% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes, which could test Celta’s late-game resilience.

sections.subtitle: Disciplinary and Set-Piece Expectations
sections.text: Discipline could play a significant role in this match, as Sevilla’s games have seen over 4.5 cards in 6 of their last 7 away matches, and 7 of their last 8 head-to-heads with Celta Vigo have also exceeded this threshold. Celta Vigo, on the other hand, have had under 4.5 cards in their last 6 home games, averaging 2.18 yellow cards per game. In terms of set-pieces, both teams trend towards low corner counts, with Celta Vigo averaging 3.82 per game (18th in the league) and 9 of their last 10 home games under 10.5 corners.

sections.subtitle: Scoring Patterns and Game Dynamics
sections.text: Celta Vigo have a tendency to score in the later stages of matches, with 25% of their goals coming between the 61st and 75th minutes. They’ve also been first to concede in 4 of their last 5 home games, which could give Sevilla early opportunities. Sevilla’s goal distribution shows a strong late-game push, with 9 goals scored in the final 15 minutes of matches. This matchup could see a cagey start but open up as the game progresses, especially if Celta exploit their attacking rankings.
